title: Dogfooding Planning Safety 935 Plus

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Finish the actionable dogfooding issues #935 and above, including durable managed-planning mutation provenance for #937 and command-backed delegation decision recording for #938.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep the provenance and delegation work package-owned, tracker-agnostic, and bounded. Preserve direct narrow-work fast paths. Use the workspace front-door CLI in user-facing commands.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Implement a minimal mutation provenance ledger, a recovery stamping command, a delegation-decision recording command, root front-door wrappers, and focused tests; update PR and issue state honestly.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A fix requires a broad planning state schema migration, external tracker coupling, or a new hidden durable state store outside package-managed planning surfaces.
canonical_core.next_action: Implement #937 and #938 with package-owned provenance and delegation-decision commands, then reconcile issues and PR scope.
proof_expectations: uv run ruff check src/agentic_workspace/cli.py packages/planning/src/repo_planning_bootstrap/installer.py tests/test_workspace_start_preflight_cli.py tests/test_workspace_summary_cli.py packages/planning/tests/test_promote.py
completion_criteria: The start and implement commands expose a planning-safety gate for broad, high-assurance, decomposed, or multi-issue work without an active execplan.
implement --changed flags implementation paths that lack active planning ownership and distinguishes planning-only from planning-plus-implementation changes.
Changed-path scope growth across multiple boundaries returns a planning escalation state with a concrete new-plan command.
Decomposition-backed work without active planning points to a concrete planning command before implementation.
Planning CLI mutations stamp managed-surface provenance and a recovery command can explicitly bless emergency manual repairs with a reason.
Planning surface checks warn for dirty managed planning surfaces that do not match CLI provenance, scoped to planning-owned files only.
A command-backed delegation decision updates the active execplan/run record and start/implement can require that decision before high-assurance decomposed implementation proceeds.
GitHub #937 and #938 are closed only if their acceptance criteria are satisfied by proof.
canonical_core.closeout_decision: archive-and-close after issue and PR reconciliation

goal: Make the #933 dogfooding failure mode harder to repeat: broad/high-assurance or decomposed implementation should require active planning ownership or return a clear violation/escalation route.
Preserve the cheap direct path for truly narrow edits.
non_goals: Do not implement a full provenance/stamping ledger for every managed planning mutation in this slice.
Do not rewrite planning installer mutation commands unless root start/implement routing cannot satisfy the actionable safety gate.
Do not claim the whole codegen epic is complete.

intent.outcome: Planning safety gate plus provenance and delegation decision completion for GitHub #935 #936 #937 #938 #939 #940.
intent.constraints: Stay within root workspace routing, planning installer/checker, CLI front door, and focused tests unless a broader schema migration becomes unavoidable.
intent.latitude: Add ledger fields, warnings, commands, next actions, and tests that make the safe path cheaper than manual edits or chat-only delegation decisions.
intent.escalation: Escalate if this requires a broad planning state schema migration or a hidden durable state store.

execution.next_step: Implement #937 and #938 with package-owned provenance and delegation-decision commands, then reconcile issues and PR scope.
execution.proof: Focused workspace CLI/planning tests, contract tooling check, selected ruff, planning checks, summary, and planning doctor.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Fix the dogfooding findings #935 and above.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Prevent planning-bypass and boundary-growth failures observed during #933.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Complete the root planning-safety gate with durable mutation provenance and command-backed delegation-decision recording.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check issue closure one by one; do not close #937 unless provenance/recovery-mode acceptance is actually implemented.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: src/agentic_workspace/cli.py; src/agentic_workspace/contracts/cli_commands.json; packages/planning/src/repo_planning_bootstrap/installer.py; packages/planning/src/repo_planning_bootstrap/cli.py; packages/planning/scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; focused workspace/planning tests; this execplan; review/PR metadata.
execution_bounds.max changed files: 12 implementation/test/planning files before generated docs or closeout artifacts.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: Focused workspace/planning pytest, contract tooling check, selected ruff, make check-planning, summary, doctor.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Stop before adding a broad planning state schema migration or external-tracker-specific provenance model.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Generated CLI packages, operation IR contracts, unrelated planning installer mutation internals, or codegen runtime surfaces.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Implement and test #937 managed-planning mutation provenance plus #938 command-backed delegation-decision recording.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Fix dogfooding planning-safety findings #935 and above.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep provenance and delegation recording bounded to package-owned planning surfaces; keep narrow direct work cheap.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Exact ledger shape, warning names, command names, tests, and issue/PR reconciliation.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A broad planning state schema migration, hidden state store, or tracker-specific coupling is required.

post_decomposition_delegation.decision rule: Keep implementation local because this change spans tightly coupled package CLI/checker/root-routing code; record this explicitly because #938 is about making skipped delegation recoverable.
post_decomposition_delegation.required evidence: Skipped manual ChatGPT relay because the user supplied the concrete target issues and acceptance criteria; local execution is cheaper without reducing proof.
post_decomposition_delegation.route skipped reason: The #937/#938 slice spans tightly coupled planning installer, checker, root routing, and contract manifests; local integration proof is cheaper than delegating a same-model worker for this pass.

system_intent_alignment.relevant system intent: Preserve the larger intended outcome separately from this bounded slice.
system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.

closure_check.reopen trigger: PR CI fails or issue reconciliation identifies an unsatisfied #937/#938 acceptance criterion.
